name: rusqlite
description: Ergonomic Rust bindings to SQLite, providing safe wrappers around the embedded SQL database

description.value: Ergonomic Rust bindings to SQLite, providing safe wrappers around the embedded SQL database
description.comment: Wraps the SQLite C library with Rust's safety guarantees, inspired by rust-postgres API

implementation.comment: Rust bindings to SQLite C library via libsqlite3-sys

first-release.comment: First published to crates.io on November 21, 2014

schema-model.comment: SQL DDL-defined schema with CREATE TABLE statements

transactions.comment: Full ACID transactions with WAL mode support

range-queries.comment: Full SQL range query support with WHERE clauses

secondary-indexes.comment: CREATE INDEX statements for secondary indexes

in-memory-mode.comment: Use ':memory:' as database path for in-memory operation

storage-architecture.comment: Each table and index stored as separate B-tree in database file with 512-65536 byte pages

compression.comment: No built-in compression; requires custom VFS extension

encryption-at-rest.comment: No native encryption; SQLCipher available via 'sqlcipher' feature flag

memory-mapped-io.comment: Optional via PRAGMA mmap_size, disabled by default

max-db-size.comment: Maximum 4,294,967,294 pages at 65,536 bytes each = ~281 terabytes

pure-rust.comment: Wraps SQLite C library via FFI bindings

async-support.comment: Synchronous API only; use tokio-rusqlite crate for async wrapper

api-safety.comment: Safe Rust wrapper around unsafe FFI calls; public API is safe

idiomatic-api.comment: Idiomatic Rust with params! macro, Result types, and iterator patterns; inspired by rust-postgres

type-safety.comment: ToSql and FromSql traits provide runtime type safety for SQL values

serde-integration.comment: Optional serde_json feature for Value type integration

no-std.comment: Requires std library due to SQLite's file I/O and system dependencies

wasm-compatible.comment: Supports wasm32-wasi via 'wasm32-wasi-vfs' feature; browser via sqlite-wasm-rs

acid-compliant.comment: Full ACID compliance; WAL mode provides additional concurrency

crash-recovery.comment: Automatic recovery on next open; durability depends on synchronous PRAGMA setting

concurrent-readers.comment: Unlimited concurrent readers in WAL mode with snapshot isolation

concurrent-writers.comment: Single writer at a time; writes serialized even in WAL mode

multi-process.comment: Multiple processes supported on same host; not over network filesystems

write-ahead-log.comment: WAL mode available via PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL since SQLite 3.7.0

maturity.comment: SQLite is extremely mature; rusqlite has 10+ years of development since 2014

ios.comment: Cross-compilation supported; use bundled feature for easier builds

android.comment: Included in Android platform's Rust crates; use bundled feature recommended

embedded.comment: Requires std and OS-level file system operations

use-cases.comment: Ideal for embedded SQL needs, local storage, and applications requiring relational queries

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads as of December 2025; top 0.5% on crates.io

maintenance-status.comment: Regular releases and active maintenance; latest release December 2025

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive API docs with examples; Rust Cookbook has tutorials; could use more guides

bus-factor.comment: Multiple active contributors; gwenn is primary maintainer with others contributing regularly
